_comment: 4-qubit ring TFIM + next-nearest-neighbor ZZ coupling (H2-style headroom problem, defined entirely numerically -- no chemistry library). H = -J*sum_ring(Z_i Z_{i+1}) - h*sum_i(X_i) - K*sum_nnn(Z_i Z_{i+2}), J=1.0 h=0.8 K=0.3 on the 4-cycle 0-1-2-3-0 with diagonals 0-2/1-3. ground_state_energy is the exact min eigenvalue of the dense 16x16 Hamiltonian (numpy.linalg.eigh, not hand-derived). The best PRODUCT (mean-field) state reaches -5.15652173913042 -- ~0.0746 Ha above E0 -- so the gap budget forces a genuinely entangled ansatz to capture the ring correlation energy. hamiltonian_terms + ground_state_energy stay hidden here; the BRIEF states the Hamiltonian shape conceptually only.
